instructions.instruction: Apply the failure-focused test to every Must item: the release fails if this is absent.
original_snippets: Challenge every `Must` using failure-focused questions.
`Must` means release failure if missing.

instructions.instruction: Must effort must not exceed 60% of total effort. Rebalance if it does.
original_snippets: Validate: Verify that the `Must` count does not exceed 60% of total effort before proceeding.
Rebalance if `Must` work exceeds 60% of effort.

instructions.instruction: Never label an item as Must solely because a stakeholder prefers it.
original_snippets: NEVER label stakeholder preference as `Must`
Preference is not the same as release-critical need.
Executive asked for dark mode, so it is Must. — BAD

instructions.instruction: Always record Won't items with an explicit revisit date.
original_snippets: NEVER skip explicit `Won't` items
Record rejected items with revisit date.
Missing exclusions invite silent scope creep.

instructions.instruction: Validate release constraints (date, budget, staffing) before assigning any categories.
original_snippets: Confirm release constraints (date, budget, team).
NEVER finalize priorities without constraint checks
Categorize before confirming release limits. — BAD

instructions.instruction: Every categorized item must include a business rationale and risk-if-omitted note.
original_snippets: NEVER keep categories without rationale
Unjustified categories cannot be defended or audited.
Include business outcome and risk rationale. — GOOD

instructions.instruction: Save the final MoSCoW output to a named file as instructed by the user.
original_snippets: Publish final table with owners and review date.

instructions.instruction: The output table must include columns: Requirement | Category | Business Rationale | Risk if Omitted.
original_snippets: | Requirement | Category | Rationale | Owner |

instructions.instruction: If Must-effort rebalancing cannot reach 60%, escalate to stakeholders to re-scope before finalizing.
original_snippets: If rebalancing cannot bring `Must` under 60%, escalate to stakeholders to either re-scope the release (split into two releases, extend the timeline, or increase capacity) before finalizing categories.

instructions.instruction: Open the categorization session by collecting all candidate requirements into a single list before sorting.
original_snippets: Collect candidate requirements in one list.
